hoses: Two equal 200 ft 2½-inch portable monitor supply lines
sceneElements.elevation: Mounted deck gun has no elevation add-on; portable monitor uses no elevation in this scenario

problems.title: Deck Gun Pump Discharge Pressure
problems.question: The deck gun is mounted directly on Engine 181 and uses 80 PSI nozzle pressure. Because the deck gun is mounted on the apparatus, do not add elevation for the deck-gun height. There is no discharge hose friction loss or appliance loss. What pump discharge pressure is required for the deck gun?
formulaBreakdown: Mounted deck gun = part of Engine 181
PDP = NP + FL + elevation + appliance loss
problems.instructorExplanation: A mounted deck gun does not receive an elevation add-on for its own height above the pump panel. With no hose friction loss and no appliance loss, PDP equals 80 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: If you entered 95 PSI, you added elevation to a mounted deck gun. Do not add elevation for the mounted deck-gun height.

problems.title: Portable Monitor Flow Per Line
problems.question: The portable monitor flows 500 GPM total and is supplied by two equal hose lines from Engine 181. The two lines split the monitor flow; they do not each flow the full monitor total. What flow should be used for each monitor supply line?
formulaBreakdown: Flow per line = total monitor flow ÷ number of lines
problems.instructorExplanation: For equal supply lines, split the total portable monitor flow between the two lines. Each line carries 250 GPM.
problems.explainMistake: Do not calculate each line at the full 500 GPM. The two lines split the flow.

problems.title: Portable Monitor Supply Line Friction Loss
problems.question: Each portable monitor supply line is 200 ft of 2½-inch hose. The portable monitor flows 500 GPM total through two equal lines, so calculate each line at one-half of the total flow. Using FL = C x (GPM/100)^2 x (length/100), with C = 2, what is the friction loss in each supply line?
problems.instructorExplanation: Each 2½-inch supply line carries 250 GPM for 200 ft, so each line has about 25 PSI friction loss.
problems.explainMistake: The most common mistake is using 500 GPM in each line instead of splitting the total flow.

problems.title: Portable Monitor Pump Discharge Pressure
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying a portable monitor through two equal 200 ft 2½-inch supply lines. The monitor flows 500 GPM total; split the flow equally between the two lines for the hose friction-loss calculation. Nozzle pressure is 80 PSI, elevation is 0 PSI, and portable monitor appliance loss is 25 PSI. What pump discharge pressure is required for the portable monitor?
formulaBreakdown: PDP = NP + FL + elevation + appliance loss
problems.instructorExplanation: The portable monitor path requires 80 PSI nozzle pressure, 25 PSI supply-line friction loss, and 25 PSI appliance loss. Total PDP is 130 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Make sure to include the portable monitor appliance loss and calculate hose friction loss per line.

problems.title: Governing Pump Discharge Pressure
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying both a mounted deck gun and a portable monitor. The deck gun uses 80 PSI nozzle pressure with no hose friction loss, no appliance loss, and no elevation add-on because it is mounted on the engine. The portable monitor flows 500 GPM total through two equal 200 ft 2½-inch supply lines. Use C = 2, split the monitor flow equally between the two lines, use 80 PSI monitor nozzle pressure, add 25 PSI monitor appliance loss, and use no elevation for the monitor. Which pump discharge pressure should govern the operation?
formulaBreakdown: Portable monitor flow per line = 500 ÷ 2 = 250 GPM
problems.instructorExplanation: The deck gun is mounted, so it stays at 80 PSI. The portable monitor has supply hose friction loss and appliance loss, so it governs at 130 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not add elevation to the mounted deck gun. Compare the deck gun PDP of 80 PSI to the portable monitor PDP of 130 PSI; the higher pressure governs.
